content.text:
<user_query>
Build a mobile-first web app called "FirstStep Korea" — 
an AI agent for international students arriving in Korea.
Positioning: "헤이영 for international students"

## TECH STACK
- Next.js 14 with App Router
- TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS
- Claude API (claude-sonnet-4-20250514) via Anthropic SDK
- Deploy-ready for Vercel

## PROJECT STRUCTURE
src/
  app/
    page.tsx                    → Landing page
    onboarding/page.tsx         → 4 questions flow
    checklist/page.tsx          → Personalized checklist
    chat/page.tsx               → AI chat with photo upload
    api/
      chat/route.ts             → Claude API endpoint
      analyze-image/route.ts    → Claude Vision endpoint
  components/
    ProgressBar.tsx
    QuestionCard.tsx
    ChecklistItem.tsx
    ChatMessage.tsx
    PhotoUpload.tsx
    UniversityBadge.tsx
  lib/
    claude.ts                   → Claude API calls
    universities.ts             → University-specific data
    checklists.ts               → Checklist logic by visa+university
  types/
    index.ts                    → TypeScript types

## TYPES (types/index.ts)
export type VisaType = 'D-2' | 'D-4' | 'E' | 'F' | 'Other'

export type University = 
  | 'Sookmyung'
  | 'Yonsei' 
  | 'Korea'
  | 'Hanyang'
  | 'Ewha'
  | 'SNU'
  | 'Other'

export type ArrivalTime = 
  | 'not-yet'
  | 'less-1-week'
  | '1-4-weeks'
  | 'more-1-month'

export type Scholarship = 
  | 'gks'
  | 'university'
  | 'none'

export interface UserProfile {
  visaType: VisaType
  university: University | null
  arrivalTime: ArrivalTime
  scholarship: Scholarship
}

export interface ChecklistItem {
  id: string
  text: string
  detail: string
  completed: boolean
  urgent?: boolean
}

export interface Message {
  role: 'user' | 'assistant'
  content: string
  imageUrl?: string
}

## PAGE 1: LANDING (app/page.tsx)
- Full screen mobile layout
- Top: "🇰🇷" large emoji
- App name: "FirstStep Korea" large bold
- Subtitle: "헤이영 for international students" 
  in gray smaller text
- Tagline: "Your personal guide to surviving 
  your first weeks in Korea"
- Description: "Banking, ARC, housing, insurance — 
  all in your language, in the right order"
- Big blue button: "Get My Personal Plan →"
  links to /onboarding
- Background: white with subtle blue gradient top
- Primary color: #185FA5

## PAGE 2: ONBOARDING (app/onboarding/page.tsx)
4 questions, one per screen with smooth transitions.
Store answers in localStorage as UserProfile.
Show progress bar at top (1/4, 2/4, 3/4, 4/4).

Question 1: "What is your visa type?"
Options as large tappable cards:
- 🎓 D-2 — University Student
- 📚 D-4 — Language Student  
- 💼 E — Work Visa
- 👨‍👩‍👧 F — Family Visa
- 🌐 Other

Question 2: "Which university?" 
ONLY show if D-2 or D-4 selected.
Options:
- Sookmyung Women's University
- Yonsei University
- Korea University
- Hanyang University
- Ewha Womans University
- SNU (Seoul National University)
- Other university

Question 3: "When did you arrive in Korea?"
Options:
- ✈️ Haven't arrived yet
- 🆕 Less than 1 week ago
- 📅 1-4 weeks ago
- ✅ More than 1 month ago

Question 4: "Do you have a scholarship?"
Options:
- 🏛️ Yes — GKS Government Scholarship
- 🎓 Yes — University Scholarship
- ❌ No Scholarship

After Q4 → redirect to /checklist

## PAGE 3: CHECKLIST (app/checklist/page.tsx)
Read UserProfile from localStorage.
Show university badge at top if university selected:
[🎓 Sookmyung Women's University]

Show personalized checklist with checkboxes.
Save completed items to localStorage.
Show progress: "3 of 8 steps completed ✓"

Button at bottom: "Ask AI Assistant →" → /chat

CHECKLIST DATA (lib/checklists.ts):
Generate checklist based on visa + university + arrival.

For D-2 + Sookmyung + less than 1 week:
[
  { text: "Visit International Office",
    detail: "순헌관 building, 1st floor. Mon-Fri 9am-5pm",
    urgent: true },
  { text: "Get temporary SIM card",
    detail: "At airport or Sinchon T-World store" },
  { text: "Pay dormitory deposit ₩300,000",
    detail: "Via IBK Bank transfer before move-in" },
  { text: "Register your address",
    detail: "At local 구청 (district office)" },
  { text: "Apply for ARC",
    detail: "Seoul Immigration Office. Need: passport + photo + address proof + ₩30,000. Within 90 days!" },
  { text: "Open bank account",
    detail: "IBK Bank on campus — bring ARC + passport" },
  { text: "Upgrade phone plan",
    detail: "Any KT/SKT/LG store with ARC" },
  { text: "Register NHIS insurance",
    detail: "Mandatory after 6 months. Missing payments affect visa renewal!" }
]

For D-2 + Yonsei + less than 1 week:
[
  { text: "Visit International Office",
    detail: "Underwood Hall, 1st floor" },
  { text: "Get SIM card",
    detail: "Sinchon area T-World or KT store" },
  { text: "Apply for ARC within 90 days",
    detail: "Seoul Immigration Office" },
  { text: "Open bank account",
    detail: "Shinhan Bank Sinchon branch — bring ARC" },
  { text: "Register address",
    detail: "서대문구청" },
  { text: "Register NHIS after 6 months",
    detail: "⚠️ Affects visa renewal if missed!" }
]

For all other combinations: generate logical checklist
based on visa type and arrival time.

## PAGE 4: CHAT (app/chat/page.tsx)

University badge at top (from localStorage).
Status: "● AI Online"

First AI message (shown automatically):
"Hi! I'm your FirstStep Korea guide 🇰🇷
I know everything about living in Korea 
as an international student.
Ask me anything in your language!"

Quick reply chips (horizontal scroll):
- 🏦 How to open a bank account?
- 🛂 How to get my ARC?
- 🏠 How to protect my deposit?
- 💸 How to send money home?
- 📋 What documents do I need?

Input row at bottom:
- 📷 Photo button (left) — opens file picker
- Text input: "Ask anything..."
- ➤ Send button (#185FA5 blue)

When photo selected: show preview in chat bubble,
send to /api/analyze-image

## API ROUTE: CHAT (app/api/chat/route.ts)

POST /api/chat
Body: { messages: Message[], userProfile: UserProfile }

System prompt:
"""
You are FirstStep Korea, an AI guide for international 
students in Korea.

Student profile:
- Visa: {visaType}
- University: {university}  
- Arrived: {arrivalTime}
- Scholarship: {scholarship}

CRITICAL RULES:
1. ALWAYS respond in the EXACT same language 
   the user writes in. Russian → Russian. 
   English → English. Uzbek → Uzbek. Never switch.
2. Be specific and practical, never generic
3. Always give the correct ORDER of steps
4. Keep responses under 150 words
5. Use numbered lists for instructions
6. Add ⚠️ for urgent warnings

UNIVERSITY-SPECIFIC INFO:
Sookmyung Women's University:
- International Office: 순헌관 1F, Mon-Fri 9am-5pm
- Best bank: IBK Bank (branch ON campus!)
- Dorm deposit: ₩300,000
- Email: international@sookmyung.ac.kr

Yonsei University:
- International Office: Underwood Hall 1F
- Best bank: Shinhan Bank Sinchon branch

Korea University:
- International Office: International Hall 1F
- Best bank: Hana Bank on campus

Hanyang University:
- International Office: Student Hall 2F

Ewha Womans University:
- International Office: Main Building 1F

CRITICAL KNOWLEDGE:
Correct order D-2 visa:
1. Temporary SIM at airport (passport only)
2. Find housing / move into dorm
3. Register address at local 구청
4. Apply for ARC (within 90 days!)
5. Open FULL bank account with ARC
6. Upgrade phone plan with ARC
7. Register NHIS after 6 months

Banking:
- Without ARC: limited account, ₩300,000 limit
- Best banks: IBK, Hana, Shinhan, Woori
- Money transfer limit: $5,000/transaction, $50,000/year
- Best transfer services: SentBe, Wise, Wirebarley

Housing:
- Always pay deposit via bank transfer
- Register lease at 주민센터 within 14 days!
- This legally protects your deposit

NHIS Insurance:
- Mandatory after 6 months
- ⚠️ Missing payments can BLOCK visa renewal
"""

## API ROUTE: IMAGE ANALYSIS (app/api/analyze-image/route.ts)

POST /api/analyze-image  
Body: { imageBase64: string, language: string }

System prompt:
"""
The user sent a photo of a Korean document.

Identify the document and respond with:
📄 This is a [document name in English and Korean]
💡 What it means: [simple explanation]
✅ What to do: [numbered steps]
📍 Where to go: [exact location or website]
⏰ Deadline: [if urgent, add ⚠️]

Documents to recognize:
- 전기요금 고지서 = electricity bill 
  → pay at GS25/CU convenience store or ATM
- 가스요금 고지서 = gas bill
  → pay at convenience store
- 건강보험료 고지서 = NHIS health insurance
  → ⚠️ affects visa renewal! pay at nhis.or.kr
- 계약서 = rental contract
  → register at 주민센터 within 14 days!
- 영수증 = receipt
- 세금 = tax document → visit local tax office
- 은행 = bank document

Always respond in {language}.
Keep response under 200 words.
"""

## DESIGN SYSTEM
Primary: #185FA5
Primary Dark: #0C447C  
Primary Light: #E6F1FB
Success: #1D9E75
Warning: #D97706
Error: #D85A30
Background: #F5F6F8
White: #FFFFFF
Text: #1A1A1A
Text Muted: #6B7280
Border: rgba(0,0,0,0.08)

Border radius: 16px cards, 12px buttons, 20px chips
Font: Inter
Max width: 390px (mobile-first, centered on desktop)
Bottom padding: 24px (safe area)

## ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES (.env.local)
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_key_here

## PACKAGE.JSON DEPENDENCIES
{
  "dependencies": {
    "next": "14.2.0",
    "react": "^18",
    "react-dom": "^18",
    "@anthropic-ai/sdk": "^0.24.0",
    "typescript": "^5",
    "tailwindcss": "^3",
    "autoprefixer": "^10",
    "postcss": "^8"
  }
}

## ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS
- All pages use 'use client' where needed
- Smooth page transitions
- Loading states for AI responses
- Error handling for API failures
- Responsive: max-width 390px centered on desktop
- GitHub-ready: include .gitignore and README.md

README.md should include:
# FirstStep Korea 🇰🇷
> 헤이영 for international students

AI agent that guides international students 
through their first weeks in Korea.

## Setup
1. Clone repo
2. npm install
3. Add ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to .env.local
4. npm run dev

## Features
- Personalized onboarding by visa type
- University-specific checklists
- AI chat in any language
- Korean document photo analysis

## Tech Stack
Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind, Claude API
</user_query>

input.contents:
# FirstStep Korea 🇰🇷

> 헤이영 for international students

AI agent that guides international students through their first weeks in Korea.

## Setup

1. Clone repo
2. `npm install`
3. Add `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` to `.env.local`
4. `npm run dev`

## Features

- Personalized onboarding by visa type
- University-specific checklists
- AI chat in any language
- Korean document photo analysis

## Tech Stack

Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind, Claude API

## Deploy (Vercel)

Set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in project Environment Variables.
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_key_here

content.text:
Here is what was added under **`/Users/sapinasamat/.cursor/projects/empty-window`** (your Cursor project root).

### What you have
- **Next.js 14.2 (App Router)**, **TypeScript**, **Tailwind 3**, **`@anthropic-ai/sdk` ^0.24.0**
- **`src/app/page.tsx`** — full-screen landing, gradient, primary **#185FA5**, CTA → `/onboarding`
- **`src/app/onboarding/page.tsx`** — 4-step flow; **university step only for D-2 / D-4** (others jump straight to arrival); progress uses **3 or 4** steps so the bar stays honest; saves **`UserProfile`** to `localStorage` → `/checklist`
- **`src/app/checklist/page.tsx`** — reads profile, **university line as `[🎓 …]`**, checklist from **`lib/checklists.ts`**, persists checkmarks, CTA → `/chat`
- **`src/app/chat/page.tsx`** — intro message, **quick chips** (horizontal scroll), **photo → `/api/analyze-image`**, text → **`/api/chat`**, loading + error UI, **max-width** comes from root layout
- **`src/app/api/chat/route.ts`** & **`src/app/api/analyze-image/route.ts`** — Claude **`claude-sonnet-4-20250514`**, system prompts as specified; chat **strips leading assistant** turns so the API always starts with a **user** message
- **Components** under `src/components/` (ProgressBar, QuestionCard, ChecklistItem, ChatMessage, PhotoUpload, UniversityBadge)
- **`src/lib/claude.ts`**, **`universities.ts`**, **`checklists.ts`** (including your **Sookmyung** and **Yonsei** <1 week lists, plus fallbacks by visa / arrival / university)
- **`src/types/index.ts`** — types only (storage keys live in **`src/lib/storage.ts`**)
- **`.gitignore`**, **`.env.example`**, **`README.md`**

### What you should run locally
`npm` was not available in this environment, so install and build on your machine:

```bash
cd /Users/sapinasamat/.cursor/projects/empty-window
npm install
cp .env.example .env.local   # then set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
npm run dev
```

### Env
Set **`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`** in **`.env.local`** (and in Vercel project settings for deploy).

If you want this in its own folder (e.g. `firststep-korea/`) or want tweaks to the **3 vs 4** progress bar copy to match “always 1/4–4/4”, say how you’d like it to read and we can align it.

# FirstStep Korea 🇰🇷

> 헤이영 for international students

AI agent that guides international students through their first weeks in Korea.

## Setup

1. Clone repo
2. `npm install`
3. Add `OPENAI_API_KEY` to `.env.local` (see `.env.example`)
4. `npm run dev`

Optional: set `OPENAI_MODEL` to a vision-capable model (defaults to `gpt-4o`).

## Features

- Personalized onboarding by visa type
- University-specific checklists
- AI chat in any language
- Korean document photo analysis

## Tech Stack

Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind, OpenAI API

## Deploy (Vercel)

Set `OPENAI_API_KEY` (and optionally `OPENAI_MODEL`) in project Environment Variables.

input.command: cd /Users/sapinasamat/.cursor/projects/empty-window && npm install && npm run build
input.description: Install openai package and verify Next.js build

content.text:
Here is what changed:

### Switched to OpenAI
- **Removed** `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and **`src/lib/claude.ts`**
- **Added** `openai` (^4.73.0) in `package.json` and **`src/lib/openai.ts`** — reads **`OPENAI_API_KEY`**, optional **`OPENAI_MODEL`** (default **`gpt-4o`** for chat + vision)
- **`/api/chat`** and **`/api/analyze-image`** now use **`openai.chat.completions.create`** with your system prompts unchanged; user messages with photos use **`image_url`** (data URLs)

### Env & docs
- **`.env.example`** — `OPENAI_API_KEY` + commented `OPENAI_MODEL`
- **`README.md`** — setup/deploy updated for OpenAI

### What you should do locally
1. `npm install` (pulls `openai`, drops Anthropic)
2. In **`.env.local`**, set **`OPENAI_API_KEY`** (you can remove **`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`**)
3. Optional: **`OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-4o-mini`** if you want a cheaper vision model (still use a **vision** model for document photos)

Restart `npm run dev` after updating env vars.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
Add a Campus Guide page to the existing FirstStep Korea app.

Add new route: /campus (app/campus/page.tsx)
Add Campus tab to bottom navigation (between Chat and Profile).

Bottom nav should now have 4 tabs:
📋 Checklist | 💬 Chat | 🎓 Campus | 👤 Profile

CAMPUS PAGE:

Header: "Campus Guide" + university badge

Search bar at top: "Search campus resources..."

Category filter chips (horizontal scroll):
All | 📄 Documents | 💰 Finance | ✍️ Academic | 🗣️ Language | 🌍 Community

Resource cards showing:
- Icon + name (English + Korean)
- Category badge
- Description
- Info rows (location, hours, price, contact)
- Warning box (red) if urgent
- Tip box (green) if helpful
- Link button if URL available

CREATE lib/campus-resources.ts with this exact data:

export const SOOKMYUNG_RESOURCES = [
  {
    id: "certificate",
    icon: "🖨️",
    name: "Certificate Issuance",
    nameKorean: "증명서 발급",
    category: "documents",
    description: "Get enrollment, transcript, graduation certificates. 3 ways: kiosk (fastest!), online (24/7), or in-person.",
    rows: [
      { label: "🏧 Kiosk", value: "학생회관 3층 로비 · Weekdays 9am-10pm · Instant!" },
      { label: "💻 Online", value: "sookmyung.ac.kr → 재학생 → 증명서발급 (24/7)" },

content.text:
Here’s what was added and how it fits together.

### New route: `/campus`
- **`src/app/(tabs)/campus/page.tsx`** — “Campus Guide” title, **university badge** (or onboarding hint), **search** (“Search campus resources…”), **horizontal category chips** (All · Documents · Finance · Academic · Language · Community), and **resource cards** via `CampusResourceCard`.

### Data: **`src/lib/campus-resources.ts`**
- Exports **`SOOKMYUNG_RESOURCES`** with the structure you specified (same fields and copy).
- Helpers: **`getCampusResourcesForUniversity`**, **`getCampusResourcesForProfile`** (only **Sookmyung** returns data for now; others get the “coming soon” / onboarding messaging).

### UI: **`src/components/CampusResourceCard.tsx`**
- Icon + English/Korean names, **category badge**, description, **rows** (label + value), optional **Price** row, **warning** (red) and **tip** (green), **“Open link”** when `url` exists.

### Bottom navigation (4 tabs)
- **`src/components/BottomNav.tsx`** — 📋 Checklist · 💬 Chat · 🎓 Campus · 👤 Profile, active state from **`usePathname()`**.
- **`src/app/(tabs)/layout.tsx`** — wraps tab screens + **`BottomNav`** in a **`min-h-dvh` flex column** so the bar stays at the bottom.

### Moved under `(tabs)` (URLs unchanged)
- **`src/app/(tabs)/checklist/page.tsx`** — removed the old fixed “Ask AI Assistant” strip (tabs cover that).
- **`src/app/(tabs)/chat/page.tsx`** — flex column + scrollable messages so the **composer sits above the tab bar** (no overlap with sticky nav).

### New: **`/profile`**
- **`src/app/(tabs)/profile/page.tsx`** — shows saved **UserProfile** and links to **onboarding** / **home** so Profile isn’t an empty tab.

### AI: **`src/app/api/chat/route.ts`**
- When **`profile.university === "Sookmyung"`**, the system prompt includes your **SOOKMYUNG CAMPUS RESOURCES** block (inserted after the Yonsei block, before Korea University).

Removed the old **`src/app/checklist/page.tsx`** and **`src/app/chat/page.tsx`** so only the `(tabs)` versions remain.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
Add a data freshness system to the FirstStep Korea app.

## GOAL
Every checklist item and campus resource should display when it was 
last verified, allow users to report outdated info in 1 tap, and 
flag stale items automatically.

## 1. NEW TYPES (add to types/index.ts)

export interface FreshnessRecord {
  itemId: string
  lastVerified: string        // ISO date string
  verifiedCount: number       // how many users confirmed it
  reportedOutdated: boolean
  reportNote?: string
}

export interface FreshnessStore {
  records: Record<string, FreshnessRecord>
  lastUpdated: string
}

## 2. NEW LIB FILE: lib/freshness.ts

Create utility functions:

- getFreshness(itemId: string): FreshnessRecord | null
  → reads from localStorage key "freshness_store"

- saveFreshness(record: FreshnessRecord): void
  → writes to localStorage

- markVerified(itemId: string): void
  → increments verifiedCount, updates lastVerified to today

- reportOutdated(itemId: string, note: string): void
  → sets reportedOutdated: true, saves note

- getDaysOld(isoDate: string): number
  → returns how many days since lastVerified

- getFreshnessLabel(itemId: string): { 
    label: string, 
    color: string, 
    emoji: string 
  }
  → returns:
    - 0-14 days: { label: "Verified recently", color: "#1D9E75", emoji: "✅" }
    - 15-30 days: { label: "Verified this month", color: "#D97706", emoji: "🟡" }  
    - 31-60 days: { label: "Verify if still accurate", color: "#D97706", emoji: "⚠️" }
    - 60+ days or no record: { label: "Needs verification", color: "#D85A30", emoji: "🔴" }

Seed initial freshness data on first load 
(localStorage key "freshness_initialized"):
Set all existing checklist item IDs and campus resource IDs 
to lastVerified = today, verifiedCount = 0.
This prevents everything showing as red on first launch.

## 3. NEW COMPONENT: components/FreshnessBadge.tsx

Props: { itemId: string, onVerify: () => void, onReport: () => void }

Renders a small row below each checklist item and campus resource card:

[✅ Verified recently · 12 days ago]  [Still accurate?  👍  ⚠️ Report]

- Left side: emoji + label + "X days ago" in muted text (font size 11px)
- Right side: two small ghost buttons:
  - 👍 "Still accurate" → calls onVerify(), shows brief "Thanks!" toast
  - ⚠️ "Report issue" → opens a small inline form with a text input 
    and submit button. On submit calls onReport(note), shows "Thanks for reporting!"
- Use primary color #185FA5
- The inline report form should be collapsible (toggle on ⚠️ click)

## 4. UPDATE: components/ChecklistItem.tsx

Import and render <FreshnessBadge> below the detail text of each item.
Pass the item.id as itemId.
On verify: call markVerified(item.id)
On report: call reportOutdated(item.id, note)

## 5. UPDATE: app/campus/page.tsx

Add <FreshnessBadge> at the bottom of each resource card.
Same behavior as checklist items.

## 6. NEW PAGE: app/admin/freshness/page.tsx

Simple read-only dashboard (no auth needed for now).
Shows a table of all tracked items:

| Item | Last Verified | Days Old | Verified By | Status | Report |
|------|--------------|----------|-------------|--------|--------|

- Sort by days old descending (stalest first)
- Row background: red tint if 60+ days, yellow if 30-60, green if <30
- Show report note if reportedOutdated is true with ⚠️ badge
- Add a "Reset all to today" button at top (for after manual review)
- Add this page link quietly in the footer of the checklist page:
  tiny gray text "Data accuracy admin →" linking to /admin/freshness

## 7. UPDATE: AI system prompt in api/chat/route.ts

Add this section:

DATA FRESHNESS AWARENESS:
- If a user asks about a specific step or resource, remind them 
  to double-check official sources as policies change.
- Add this footer to any response about documents, fees, or deadlines:
  "💡 Double-check this at the official source — rules can change 
  each semester."
- Never say information is "definitely current" or "guaranteed accurate."

## DESIGN RULES
- FreshnessBadge must be subtle — small text, no visual noise
- Don't block the user flow — verification is optional, never a gate
- Toast messages: small bottom toast, auto-dismiss after 2 seconds
- All freshness data stays in localStorage (no backend needed)
- Use existing design tokens: #185FA5, #1D9E75, #D97706, #D85A30
</user_query>

content.text:
Here’s what was added and how it behaves.

### Types (`src/types/index.ts`)
- **`FreshnessRecord`** — `itemId`, `lastVerified`, `verifiedCount`, `reportedOutdated`, optional `reportNote`
- **`FreshnessStore`** — `records`, `lastUpdated`

### `src/lib/freshness.ts`
- **`getFreshness` / `saveFreshness`** — read/write **`freshness_store`**
- **`markVerified`** — bumps **`verifiedCount`**, sets **`lastVerified`** to now, clears **`reportedOutdated`** / **`reportNote`**
- **`reportOutdated`** — sets **`reportedOutdated`** + **`reportNote`**
- **`getDaysOld`** — calendar days since **`lastVerified`** (UTC dates)
- **`getFreshnessLabel`** — buckets 0–14 / 15–30 / 31–60 / 61+ or missing → labels, colors, emojis as specified
- **`ensureFreshnessInitialized`** — if **`freshness_initialized`** is missing: seeds **all** checklist IDs (**`ALL_TRACKED_CHECKLIST_ITEM_IDS`** from `checklists.ts`) + **`TRACKED_CAMPUS_RESOURCE_IDS`** with **`lastVerified = now`**, **`verifiedCount = 0`**, then sets **`freshness_initialized`**
- **`resetAllFreshnessToToday`** — admin helper
- **`getAdminFreshnessRows`** — sorted by **`daysOld` descending**

### `src/lib/checklists.ts`
- **`ALL_TRACKED_CHECKLIST_ITEM_IDS`** — union of every seeded checklist id (via `Set`).

### `src/lib/campus-resources.ts`
- **`TRACKED_CAMPUS_RESOURCE_IDS`** — ids from **`SOOKMYUNG_RESOURCES`** (declared after the array).

### `src/components/FreshnessBadge.tsx`
- Compact **[✅ … · X days ago]**, **Still accurate?**, **👍** (with `aria-label`), **⚠️ Report**
- Collapsible report UI with **`input type="text"`** + Submit / Cancel
- **`fixed`** toast (~**2s**): “Thanks!” / “Thanks for reporting!”

### `FreshnessInit` + tabs layout
- **`FreshnessInit`** runs **`ensureFreshnessInitialized()`** once when tab shell mounts (`src/app/(tabs)/layout.tsx`).

### `ChecklistItem` & `CampusResourceCard`
- Checkbox/card layout adjusted so buttons aren’t inside a **`label`**
- **`FreshnessBadge`** wired with **`markVerified` / `reportOutdated`**

### Checklist footer
- Small gray **“Data accuracy admin →”** → **`/admin/freshness`** (with checklist content **and** the empty-profile state).

### Admin **`/admin/freshness`**
- Table + stale-first sort + row tint (**<30** green, **30–59** yellow, **60+** red)
- Report notes + **⚠️ Flagged** when **`reportedOutdated`**
- **Reset all to today** (confirm dialog)

### AI (`src/app/api/chat/route.ts`)
- **`DATA FRESHNESS AWARENESS`** block added per your copy.

**Note:** To fully reset freshness behavior during testing, remove **`freshness_store`** and **`freshness_initialized`** from DevTools → Application → Local Storage so seeding runs again.

[REDACTED]
